evals.name: api-dockerfile-multi-target
evals.prompt: Create `infra/api.Dockerfile` for a FastAPI service at `apps/api/`. Stack: Python 3.12, uv as the package manager (apps/api/pyproject.toml + apps/api/uv.lock), asyncpg. The build context will be the repo root (not infra/). I need this Dockerfile to support: (a) hot reload during local dev, (b) running pytest in-container for CI, (c) production deployment as a non-root user with proper signal handling and a PORT that the platform injects. Write the complete file to infra/api.Dockerfile.
evals.expected_output: A multi-target Dockerfile (named stages: builder, test, dev, prod) using BuildKit cache mounts for uv, layered with deps before code, non-root user with explicit UID in prod, healthcheck, exec-form CMD with PORT env var support, slim-bookworm (not alpine), uv installed via the upstream image (not curl|sh).

assertions.description: Uses BuildKit cache mount for uv
Does NOT install uv via curl|sh

assertions.description_long: The line copying pyproject.toml/uv.lock should appear before the line copying src/

evals.name: compose-with-healthcheck-deps
evals.prompt: Write `infra/docker-compose.yml` plus a `infra/docker-compose.override.yml`. Three services: db (postgres:16 with init.sql mounted at /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql, persistent volume), api (built from infra/api.Dockerfile, build context = repo root, target=prod by default but switched to dev in the override), and web (built from infra/web.Dockerfile, similar target switch). The api must NOT start until db is ready (use a real healthcheck condition, not just service_started). In dev mode (override) both api and web should hot-reload — bind-mount source from apps/api/src and apps/web. The api needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from a .env file or shell — fail clearly if it's missing.
evals.expected_output: Two compose files. Base: db with pg_isready healthcheck, api with depends_on db service_healthy + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY interpolated with :? guard, web. Override: target: dev for api+web, source bind-mounts, --reload for uvicorn, vite dev command for web, anonymous volume for /app/node_modules, port mapping override for vite (5173:5173 instead of 5173:80).

assertions.description: Produces both base + override compose files

evals.name: gha-deploy-cloud-run-wif
evals.prompt: Add a GitHub Actions workflow at `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` that, on push to main, builds the api image (from infra/api.Dockerfile, target=prod, platform linux/amd64), pushes it to **Google Artifact Registry**, and deploys to Google Cloud Run. Auth must use **Workload Identity Federation** — no JSON service account keys. The deploy step must wire `--add-cloudsql-instances` for a Cloud SQL Postgres instance and pull `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from Secret Manager via `--set-secrets`. Use BuildKit GHA cache for fast incremental builds. Tag with both the commit SHA and `latest`.
evals.expected_output: A GitHub Actions workflow with id-token: write permission, google-github-actions/auth@v2 using workload_identity_provider + service_account, setup-gcloud, configure-docker for Artifact Registry (REGION-docker.pkg.dev), docker/build-push-action@v6 with target=prod and cache-from/to: type=gha, then a gcloud run deploy step with --add-cloudsql-instances and --set-secrets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...:latest. SHA-tagged via metadata-action.

assertions.description: Does NOT use credentials_json or service-account JSON keys

evals.name: cloud-run-readiness-review
evals.prompt:
Review this Dockerfile for a FastAPI api and tell me everything that needs to change for it to deploy cleanly to a serverless container platform like Google Cloud Run. The project uses uv for dependency management. Be specific about each issue and explain why each change matters.

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.12
COPY . /app
WORKDIR /app
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt
ENV ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-REDACTED-example-do-not-leak
EXPOSE 5000
CMD python main.py
```
evals.expected_output: A review identifying: single-stage (should be multi-stage), no slim/digest pin, COPY . . without .dockerignore, pip not uv (project uses uv), root user (no non-root user), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY baked into image (critical security issue — must use a secret store / --set-secrets at deploy time), wrong port (5000 vs PORT env var injection), shell-form CMD breaks SIGTERM, binds to localhost (not 0.0.0.0), no healthcheck, no BuildKit cache mounts. Each issue should explain WHY (platform constraint, security implication, performance).

assertions.description: Flags baked-in ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as a security issue
Flags need to use PORT env var
Explains WHY each issue matters (platform constraint, security, etc.)
